evals.prompt: Audit the research corpus for coverage gaps against the config-declared dimension taxonomy. The fixed sample corpus holds exactly one finding per dimension, so it cannot exercise gap detection — build a deliberately skewed corpus first. Create a findings directory `skewed/` containing TWO MIF findings whose `extensions.harness.dimension` is `technical` (copy the JSON shape from `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings/finding-copier.json`, give each a distinct `@id`, and set the dimension to `technical`). Build its index: `bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-index.sh skewed skewed-index.json`. Then read the authoritative dimension taxonomy from `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/harness.config.json` (`.dimensions[].id`), count findings per dimension in `skewed-index.json`, and emit the coverage rollup. Write a JSON object `{"coverage": [ {"dimension": <id>, "findings": <count>} ... ]}` covering every config dimension to `gaps.json`, and a readable coverage table that flags the zero-finding dimensions to `gaps.md`.
evals.expected_output: gaps.json whose coverage array lists technical with 2 findings and landscape, trajectory, and market with 0 findings each, plus gaps.md that surfaces landscape, trajectory, and market as coverage gaps and recommends filling them, while NOT flagging technical (which is populated).

deterministic_checks.description: coverage rollup JSON was written
gaps.json is valid JSON
coverage rollup covers all four config dimensions
landscape is a true zero-finding gap in the skewed corpus
trajectory is a true zero-finding gap in the skewed corpus
market is a true zero-finding gap in the skewed corpus
technical is populated (2 findings) and must NOT be flagged as a gap
the gap report names the landscape dimension
the gap report names the trajectory dimension
the gap report names the market dimension

expectations: gaps.md identifies landscape, trajectory, and market as the coverage gaps (zero findings) and does not present technical as a gap.
The report recommends a concrete next step for the empty dimensions (e.g. running /start --augment to fill them), per the skill's guidance.
The dimension taxonomy is read from harness.config.json, not hard-coded — all four configured dimensions appear in the rollup.

evals.prompt: Find the clusters in the research corpus — sets of findings tied together through the MIF-native knowledge graph by a shared referenced entity. Read the graph at `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/knowledge-graph.json`, group the `via: "entity"` (mentions) edges by their target entity, and keep only entities referenced by more than one finding. Write a JSON object `{"clusters": [ {"entity": <urn>, "findings": [<finding ids>], "size": <n>} ... ]}` sorted by descending size to `clusters.json`, and a readable summary naming each cluster by its entity label to `clusters.md`.
evals.expected_output: clusters.json with exactly one cluster — the entity urn:mif:entity:concept:update-propagation, shared by all three findings (size 3) — and clusters.md naming the 'update propagation' cluster. The single-finding entities (cookiecutter, copier, living-template) must NOT appear as clusters.

deterministic_checks.description: cluster JSON was written
clusters.json is valid JSON
exactly one shared-entity cluster exists in the corpus
the cluster is the update-propagation entity shared by all 3 findings
no second cluster exists — single-finding entities are not clusters
the cluster is named by its entity label

expectations: Exactly one cluster is reported, keyed on the update-propagation entity that all three findings mention.
Entities referenced by only one finding (Cookiecutter, Copier, living template) are correctly excluded from the cluster list.
clusters.md highlights that this cluster bridges findings of different dimensions (technical, landscape, trajectory) — a cross-cutting theme.

evals.prompt: Report the stale findings in the corpus. Staleness has two independent signals. (1) Verdict-based: read `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/research-index.json` and collect findings whose `verdict` is anything other than `survived` (and non-null) — these are epistemically stale regardless of age. (2) Age-based: read the MIF findings at `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings/*.json` and list their `created` dates oldest-first. Write a JSON object `{"verdict_stale": [ <ids> ], "age_stale": [ {"id": <id>, "created": <date>} ... ]}` to `stale.json`, and a readable report to `stale.md`.
evals.expected_output: stale.json with an EMPTY verdict_stale array (all three sample findings are survived) and an age_stale list covering the three findings with their 2026-06-19 created dates. stale.md states there are no verdict-based stale findings and lists the findings by age, without falsely flagging any survived finding as weakened/falsified/quarantined.

deterministic_checks.description: stale report JSON was written
stale.json is valid JSON
no verdict-based stale findings — all three findings are survived
the age list covers all three findings
the age-based section surfaces the findings' created date

expectations: The verdict-based stale section is empty and the report says so explicitly — no survived finding is mislabeled weakened, falsified, or quarantined.
The age-based section lists all three findings ordered by their created date.
Each listed finding carries a recommendation (e.g. /update to refresh or /falsify to re-test), per the skill's output spec.

evals.prompt: Run the full corpus audit with `/discover --all` over the sample session at `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/` (index `research-index.json`, graph `knowledge-graph.json`, findings dir, and dimensions from `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/harness.config.json`). Produce the three headed sections — Coverage gaps, Clusters, and Stale findings — followed by a one-line summary, and write the complete report to `audit.md`.
evals.expected_output: audit.md containing all three section headers (Coverage gaps, Clusters, Stale findings) and a one-line summary in the form 'N gaps, M clusters, P stale findings'.

deterministic_checks.description: the combined audit report was written
the report has a Coverage gaps section
the report has a Clusters section
the report has a Stale findings section
the report ends with the summary line in the documented format
the Clusters section surfaces the real update-propagation cluster

expectations: All three analyses appear as distinct headed sections in the single report.
The summary line correctly reflects the corpus: against the fixed (unskewed) sample, the clusters count is 1 (update propagation) and the verdict-based stale count is 0.
The Clusters section names the update-propagation cross-cutting theme rather than recomputing tag co-occurrence.

evals.prompt: Check coverage gaps on a balanced corpus where every config-declared dimension has exactly one finding — the normal healthy state. Read the dimension taxonomy from $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/harness.config.json (`.dimensions[].id`) and build a findings directory `balanced/` containing exactly ONE MIF finding per config dimension (copy the JSON shape from `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings/finding-copier.json`, give each a distinct `@id`, and set `extensions.harness.dimension` to each dimension id in turn). Build its index: `bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-index.sh balanced balanced-index.json`. Write the gap-coverage rollup as `{"coverage": [{"dimension": <id>, "findings": <count>}]}` to `gaps-balanced.json` and a readable table to `gaps-balanced.md`.
evals.expected_output: gaps-balanced.json showing every config dimension (technical, landscape, trajectory, market) with exactly 1 finding. No dimension has 0 findings. gaps-balanced.md contains all four dimensions in the table and does NOT flag any of them as a gap or emit /start --augment recommendations.

deterministic_checks.description: coverage rollup for balanced corpus was written
gaps-balanced.json is valid JSON
all four config-declared dimensions appear in the output
technical has exactly 1 finding in the balanced corpus
landscape has exactly 1 finding in the balanced corpus
trajectory has exactly 1 finding in the balanced corpus
market has exactly 1 finding in the balanced corpus
no dimension has 0 findings — the balanced corpus has no genuine gaps

expectations: No dimension is flagged as a coverage gap — each of the four config-declared dimensions has exactly 1 finding and none is at 0 or far below the corpus mean.
No /start --augment commands are recommended because the corpus is uniformly balanced.

evals.prompt: Verify that verdict-based stale detection fires correctly when a weakened finding exists. First create directory ./stale-test-findings/ and copy $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/reports/_meta/sample-session/findings/finding-copier.json there as `finding-survived.json`. Then write a second MIF finding to ./stale-test-findings/finding-weakened.json with: `@id` = `urn:mif:concept:harness:kg-weakened-test`, `title` = `Test weakened finding`, `created` = `2025-01-15T12:00:00Z`, `namespace` = `harness/test`, `extensions.harness.dimension` = `technical`, `extensions.harness.verification.verdict` = `weakened`, `extensions.harness.verification.verdict_basis` = `Evidence partially refuted by counter-source`, and empty `entities`, `citations`, `tags` arrays (follow the schema from finding-copier.json for all other required fields). Build the index: `bash $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/scripts/build-index.sh ./stale-test-findings ./stale-test-index.json`. Run the stale analysis against `./stale-test-index.json` and the finding files in `./stale-test-findings/`. Write structured output to `stale-weakened.json` as `{"verdict_stale": [{"id": "...", "title": "...", "verdict": "...", "verdict_basis": "..."}], "age_stale": [{"id": "...", "created": "..."}]}` and a readable report to `stale-weakened.md`.
evals.expected_output: stale-weakened.json with verdict_stale containing exactly 1 entry — urn:mif:concept:harness:kg-weakened-test — and the survived copier finding absent from verdict_stale. The age_stale list shows 2025-01-15 as an older date than 2026-06-19.

deterministic_checks.description: stale report JSON for the weakened corpus was written
stale-weakened.json is valid JSON
exactly one verdict-stale finding detected — the weakened finding
the weakened verdict finding appears in verdict_stale
the stale entry ID matches the manufactured weakened finding, not the survived copier
the stale report names the weakened verdict
the older created date (2025) surfaces in the age-based section

expectations: urn:mif:concept:harness:kg-weakened-test is flagged in the verdict-stale section with its verdict_basis ('Evidence partially refuted by counter-source') and a /update or /falsify recommendation.
The survived copier finding does NOT appear in verdict_stale — only the weakened finding is verdict-stale.
The age list shows 2025-01-15 as older than 2026-06-19, identifying the weakened finding as the most urgently stale.
